title: Exception: outstanding options granted to employees
text: Provided that the provisions of this sub-regulation shall not apply to outstanding options granted to employees, whether currently an employee or not, pursuant to an employee stock option scheme in compliance with the Companies Act, 2013, the relevant Guidance Note or accounting standards, if any, issued by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India or pursuant to the Companies Act, 2013, in this regard.

source.reg: Entities not eligible to make an initial public offer
source.span_hint: outstanding options granted

title: Exception: outstanding stock appreciation rights (SAR)
text: Provided that the provisions of this sub-regulation shall not apply to outstanding stock appreciation rights granted to employees pursuant to a stock appreciation right scheme, which are fully exercised for equity shares prior to the filing of the red herring prospectus (in case of book-built issues) or the prospectus (in case of fixed price issues), as the case may be, disclosures regarding such stock appreciation rights and the scheme and the total number of equity shares resulting from the exercise of such rights are made in the draft offer document and offer document.

source.reg: Entities not eligible to make an initial public offer
source.span_hint: outstanding stock appreciation rights

title: Exception: fully paid-up outstanding convertible securities
text: Provided that the provisions of this sub-regulation shall not apply to fully paid-up outstanding convertible securities which are required to be converted on or before the date of filing of the red herring prospectus (in case of book-built issues) or the prospectus (in case of fixed price issues), as the case may be.

source.reg: Entities not eligible to make an initial public offer
source.span_hint: fully paid-up outstanding convertible securities

title: Technology-intensive requirement for SR equity shares
text: the issuer shall be intensive in the use of technology, information technology, intellectual property, data analytics, bio-technology or nano-technology to provide products, services or business platforms with substantial value addition.

source.span_hint: the issuer shall be intensive in the use of technology

title: Net worth requirement for SR shareholder
text: the net worth of the SR shareholder, as determined by a Registered Valuer, shall not be more than rupees one thousand crore.

source.span_hint: the net worth of the SR shareholder

title: SR shares issuance to promoters/founders
text: The SR shares were issued only to the promoters/ founders who hold an executive position in the issuer company;

source.span_hint: The SR shares were issued only to the promoters/ founders

title: Holding period requirement for SR equity shares
text: the SR equity shares have been issued prior to the filing of draft red herring prospectus and held for a period of at least three months prior to the filing of the red herring prospectus;

source.span_hint: the SR equity shares have been issued prior

title: Voting rights ratio for SR equity shares
text: The SR equity shares shall have voting rights in the ratio of a minimum of 2:1 upto a maximum of 10:1 compared to ordinary shares and such ratio shall be in whole numbers only;

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall have voting rights

title: Face value requirement for SR equity shares
text: The SR equity shares shall have the same face value as the ordinary shares;

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall have the same face value

title: Single class of SR equity shares requirement
text: The issuer shall only have one class of SR equity shares;

source.span_hint: The issuer shall only have one class of SR equity shares

title: Equivalence to ordinary shares for SR equity shares
text: The SR equity shares shall be equivalent to ordinary equity shares in all respects, except for having superior voting rights.

source.span_hint: The SR equity shares shall be equivalent

title: Application for in-principle approval to list specified securities
text: An issuer making an initial public offer shall ensure that it has made an application to one or more stock exchanges to seek an in-principle approval for listing of its specified securities on such stock exchanges and has chosen one of them as the designated stock exchange, in terms of Schedule XIX.
notes: Map to issuer's application status with stock exchanges.

source.span_hint: application to one or more stock exchanges

title: Agreement with depository for dematerialisation
text: An issuer making an initial public offer shall ensure that it has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialisation of the specified securities already issued and proposed to be issued.

source.span_hint: entered into an agreement with a depository

title: Promoters' securities in dematerialised form
text: An issuer making an initial public offer shall ensure that all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form prior to filing of the offer document.
notes: Map to promoter holdings and their dematerialisation status.

source.span_hint: all its specified securities held by the promoters

title: Partly paid-up equity shares fully paid-up or forfeited
text: An issuer making an initial public offer shall ensure that all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up or have been forfeited.
notes: Map to status of partly paid-up equity shares.

source.span_hint: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares

title: Firm arrangements for project finance
text: An issuer making an initial public offer shall ensure that it has made firm arrangements of finance through verifiable means towards seventy five per cent. of the stated means of finance for a specific project proposed to be funded from the issue proceeds, excluding the amount to be raised through the proposed public issue or through existing identifiable internal accruals.
notes: Map to financial arrangements and project funding details.

source.span_hint: firm arrangements of finance through verifiable means

title: General corporate purposes limit (25%)
text: The amount for general corporate purposes, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.

source.span_hint: The amount for general corporate purposes

title: Definition of project
text: "project" means the object for which monies are proposed to be raised to cover the objects of the issue;

source.span_hint: "project" means the object for which monies

title: Limit for unidentified acquisition/investment (25%)
text: Provided that the amount raised for such objects where the issuer company has not identified acquisition or investment target, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document, shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer:

source.span_hint: Provided that the amount raised for such objects

title: Holding period of equity shares (1 year)
text: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered for sale to the public, which have been held by the sellers for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document:

source.span_hint: Only such fully paid-up equity shares may be offered

title: Holding period for convertible securities (1 year)
text: Provided that in case the equity shares received on conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities including depository receipts are being offered for sale, the holding period of such convertible securities, including depository receipts, as well as that of resultant equity shares together shall be considered for the purpose of calculation of one year period referred in this sub-regulation.

source.span_hint: Provided that in case the equity shares received

title: Offer for sale of government company/statutory authority infrastructure sector
text: In case of an offer for sale of a government company or statutory authority or corporation or any special purpose vehicle set up and controlled by any one or more of them, which is engaged in the infrastructure sector.

source.span_hint: in case of an offer for sale

title: Equity shares acquired pursuant to High Court/tribunal/Central Government scheme
text: If the equity shares offered for sale were acquired pursuant to any scheme approved by a High Court or approved by a tribunal or the Central Government under sections 230 to 234 of Companies Act, 2013, as applicable, in lieu of business and invested capital which had been in existence for a period of more than one year prior to approval of such scheme.

source.span_hint: if the equity shares offered for sale

title: Equity shares issued under bonus issue on securities held for at least one year
text: If the equity shares offered for sale were issued under a bonus issue on securities held for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document with the Board and further subject to the following: (i) such specified securities being issued out of free reserves and share premium existing in the books of account as at the end of the financial year preceding the financial year in which the draft offer document is filed with the Board; and (ii) such equity shares not being issued by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealized profits of the issuer.

source.span_hint: if the equity shares offered for sale

title: Eligibility for issue of convertible debt instruments
text: An issuer shall be eligible to make an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments even without making a prior public issue of its equity shares and listing thereof, provided that it is not in default of payment of interest or repayment of principal amount in respect of debt instruments issued by it to the public, if any, for a period of more than six months.

source.span_hint: An issuer shall be eligible

title: Credit rating requirement for convertible debt instruments
text: In addition to other requirements laid down in these regulations, an issuer making an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments shall also comply with the following conditions: it has obtained credit rating from at least one credit rating agency.

source.span_hint: it has obtained credit rating

title: Debenture trustee requirement for convertible debt instruments
text: In addition to other requirements laid down in these regulations, an issuer making an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments shall also comply with the following conditions: it has appointed at least one debenture trustee in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993.

source.span_hint: it has appointed at least one

title: Debenture redemption reserve requirement for convertible debt instruments
text: In addition to other requirements laid down in these regulations, an issuer making an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments shall also comply with the following conditions: it shall create a debenture redemption reserve in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder.

source.span_hint: it shall create a debenture

title: Credit rating requirement for convertible debt issue
text: it has obtained credit rating from at least one credit rating agency;

source.reg: Additional requirements for issue of convertible debt instruments
source.span_hint: it has obtained credit rating

title: Debenture trustee requirement for convertible debt issue
text: it has appointed at least one debenture trustee in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Debenture Trustees) Regulations, 1993;

source.reg: Additional requirements for issue of convertible debt instruments
source.span_hint: it has appointed at least one debenture trustee

title: Debenture redemption reserve requirement for convertible debt issue
text: it shall create a debenture redemption reserve in accordance with the provisions of the Companies Act, 2013 and rules made thereunder;

source.reg: Additional requirements for issue of convertible debt instruments
source.span_hint: it shall create a debenture redemption reserve

title: Redemption of convertible debt instruments
text: The issuer shall redeem the convertible debt instruments in terms of the offer document.

source.reg: Additional requirements for issue of convertible debt instruments
source.span_hint: The issuer shall redeem

title: Positive consent required for conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments
text: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares unless the holders of such convertible debt instruments have sent their positive consent to the issuer and non-receipt of reply to any notice sent by the issuer for this purpose shall not be construed as consent for conversion of any convertible debt instruments.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: The issuer shall not convert

title: Option to holders for conversion of listed convertible debt instruments
text: Where the value of the convertible portion of any listed convertible debt instruments issued by an issuer exceeds ten crore rupees and the issuer has not determined the conversion price of such convertible debt instruments at the time of making the issue, the holders of such convertible debt instruments shall be given the option of not converting the convertible portion into equity shares: Provided that where the upper limit on the price of such convertible debt instruments and justification thereon is determined and disclosed to the investors at the time of making the issue, it shall not be necessary to give such option to the holders of the convertible debt instruments for converting the convertible portion into equity share capital within the said upper limit.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: Where the value of the convertible portion

title: Redemption within one month for non-exercised conversion option
text: Where an option is to be given to the holders of the convertible debt instruments in terms of sub-regulation (2) and if one or more of such holders do not exercise the option to convert the instruments into equity share capital at a price determined in the general meeting of the shareholders, the issuer shall redeem that part of the instruments within one month from the last date by which option is to be exercised, at a price which shall not be less than its face value.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: Where an option is to be given

title: Redemption as per disclosures in offer document
text: The provision of sub-regulation (2) shall not apply if such redemption is as per the disclosures made in the offer document.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: The provision of sub-regulation (2)

title: Restriction on issuing convertible debt for financing promoter group or group companies
text: An issuer shall not issue convertible debt instruments for financing or for providing loans to or for acquiring shares of any person who is part of the promoter group or group companies: Provided that an issuer shall be eligible to issue fully convertible debt instruments for these purposes if the period of conversion of such debt instruments is less than eighteen months from the date of issue of such debt instruments.

source.reg: Issue of convertible debt instruments for financing
source.span_hint: An issuer shall not issue

title: Exception to option for conversion with upper limit on price
text: Provided that where the upper limit on the price of such convertible debt instruments and justification thereon is determined and disclosed to the investors at the time of making the issue, it shall not be necessary to give such option to the holders of the convertible debt instruments for converting the convertible portion into equity share capital within the said upper limit.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: Provided that where the upper limit on the price

title: Exception to restriction on issuing fully convertible debt instruments for financing or loans
text: Provided that an issuer shall be eligible to issue fully convertible debt instruments for these purposes if the period of conversion of such debt instruments is less than eighteen months from the date of issue of such debt instruments.

source.reg: Issue of convertible debt instruments for financing
source.span_hint: Provided that an issuer shall be eligible

title: Tenure of warrants in initial public offer
text: The tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: The tenure of such warrants shall not exceed

title: Attachment of one or more warrants to specified security
text: A specified security may have one or more warrants attached to it.

source.span_hint: a specified security may have one or more

title: Determination and disclosure of exercise price for warrants
text: The price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and disclosed in the offer document and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront.

source.span_hint: The price or formula for determination

title: Forfeiture of consideration for unexercised warrants
text: In case the warrant holder does not exercise the option to take equity shares against any of the warrants held by the warrant holder, within three months from the date of payment of consideration, such consideration made in respect of such warrants shall be forfeited by the issuer.

source.span_hint: In case the warrant holder does not exercise

title: Minimum promoters’ contribution requirement
text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital.

source.span_hint: The promoters of the issuer shall hold

title: Alternative contributors to meet shortfall in minimum contribution
text: Provided that in case the post-issue shareholding of the promoters is less than twenty per cent., alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India 37[or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s)] may contribute to meet the shortfall in minimum contribution as specified for the promoters, subject to a maximum of ten per cent. of the post-issue capital without being identified as promoter(s).

source.span_hint: Provided that in case the post-issue shareholding

title: Minimum Promoters' Contribution
text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital: Provided that in case the post-issue shareholding of the promoters is less than twenty per cent., alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India 37[or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s)] may contribute to meet the shortfall in minimum contribution as specified for the promoters, subject to a maximum of ten per cent. of the post-issue capital without being identified as promoter(s). Provided further that the requirement of minimum promoters’ contribution shall not apply in case an issuer does not have any identifiable promoter.

source.span_hint: The promoters of the issuer shall hold

title: Promoters' Contribution by Equity or Convertible Securities
text: The promoters shall contribute twenty per cent. as stipulated in sub-regulation (1), as the case may be, either by way of equity shares38[, including SR equity shares held, if any,] or by way of subscription to convertible securities: Provided that if the price of the equity shares allotted pursuant to conversion is not pre-determined and not disclosed in the offer document, the promoters shall contribute only by way of subscription to the convertible securities being issued in the public issue and shall undertake in writing to subscribe to the equity shares pursuant to conversion of such securities.

source.span_hint: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent.

title: Price of Convertible Securities
text: In case of any issue of convertible securities which are convertible or exchangeable on different dates and if the promoters’ contribution is by way of equity shares (conversion price being pre-determined), such contribution shall not be at a price lower than the weighted average price of the equity share capital arising out of conversion of such securities.

source.span_hint: In case of any issue of convertible securities

title: Initial Public Offer Contribution for Convertible Debt Instruments
text: Subject to the provisions of clause (a) and (b) above, in case of an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments without a prior public issue of equity shares, the promoters shall bring in a contribution of at least twenty per cent. of the project cost in the form of equity shares, subject to contributing at least twenty per cent. of the issue size from their own funds in the form of equity shares: Provided that if the project is to be implemented in stages, the promoters’ contribution shall be with respect to total equity participation till the respective stage vis-à-vis the debt raised or proposed to be raised through the public issue.
notes: Map to convertible_debt_instruments_contribution >= 20% of project cost and issue size.

source.span_hint: Subject to the provisions of clause (a) and (b)

title: Satisfying Contribution Requirements Prior to Issue Opening
text: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements of this regulation at least one day prior to the date of opening of the issue.

source.span_hint: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements

title: Escrow Account for Promoters' Contribution
text: In case the promoters have to subscribe to equity shares or convertible securities towards minimum promoters’ contribution, the amount of promoters’ contribution shall be kept in an escrow account with a scheduled commercial bank, which shall be released to the issuer along with the release of the issue proceeds: Provided that where the promoters’ contribution has already been brought in and utilised, the issuer shall give the cash flow statement disclosing the use of such funds in the offer document; Provided further that where the minimum promoters’ contribution is more than one hundred crore rupees and the initial public offer is for partly paid shares, the promoters shall bring in at least one hundred crore rupees before the date of opening of the issue and the remaining amount may be brought on a pro-rata basis before the calls are made to the public.

source.span_hint: In case the promoters have to subscribe

title: Promoters' Contribution: Equity or Convertible Securities
text: The promoters shall contribute twenty per cent., as stipulated in sub-regulation (1), either by way of equity shares, including SR equity shares held, if any, or by way of subscription to convertible securities.

source.span_hint: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent.

title: Promoters' Contribution: Weighted Average Price
text: In case of any issue of convertible securities which are convertible or exchangeable on different dates and if the promoters’ contribution is by way of equity shares (conversion price being pre-determined), such contribution shall not be at a price lower than the weighted average price of the equity share capital arising out of conversion of such securities.

source.span_hint: In case of any issue of convertible securities

title: Promoters' Contribution: Initial Public Offer of Convertible Debt Instruments
text: Subject to the provisions of clause (a) and (b) above, in case of an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments without a prior public issue of equity shares, the promoters shall bring in a contribution of at least twenty per cent. of the project cost in the form of equity shares, subject to contributing at least twenty per cent. of the issue size from their own funds in the form of equity shares.

source.span_hint: Subject to the provisions of clause (a) and (b)

title: Promoters' Contribution: Satisfaction Prior to Issue Opening
text: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements of this regulation at least one day prior to the date of opening of the issue.

source.span_hint: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements of this regulation

title: Promoters' Contribution: Escrow Account Requirement
text: In case the promoters have to subscribe to equity shares or convertible securities towards minimum promoters’ contribution, the amount of promoters’ contribution shall be kept in an escrow account with a scheduled commercial bank, which shall be released to the issuer along with the release of the issue proceeds.

source.span_hint: In case the promoters have to subscribe to equity shares

title: Promoters' Contribution: Cash Flow Statement Disclosure
text: Provided that where the promoters’ contribution has already been brought in and utilised, the issuer shall give the cash flow statement disclosing the use of such funds in the offer document.

source.span_hint: Provided that where the promoters’ contribution has already been brought in

title: Specified securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution (I)
text: For the computation of minimum promoters' contribution, specified securities acquired during the preceding three years are not eligible if they are: (i) acquired for consideration other than cash and revaluation of assets or capitalisation of intangible assets is involved in such transaction; or (ii) resulting from a bonus issue by utilisation of revaluation reserves or unrealised profits of the issuer or from bonus issue against equity shares which are ineligible for minimum promoters' contribution.
notes: Map to acquisition_details and transaction_type fields.

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: For the computation of minimum

title: Specified securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution (II)
text: For the computation of minimum promoters' contribution, specified securities acquired by the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s), during the preceding one year at a price lower than the price at which specified securities are being offered to the public in the initial public offer, are not eligible.
notes: Map to acquisition_details and entity_type fields.

source.reg: Securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: specified securities acquired by the

title: Difference Payment for Specified Securities
text: if the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s), as applicable, pay to the issuer the difference between the price at which the specified securities are offered in the initial public offer and the price at which the specified securities had been acquired;

source.span_hint: if the promoters and alternative investment funds

title: Acquisition of Specified Securities under Scheme
text: if such specified securities are acquired in terms of the scheme under sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act, 2013, as approved by a High Court or a tribunal or the Central Government, as applicable, by the promoters in lieu of business and invested capital that had been in existence for a period of more than one year prior to such approval;

source.span_hint: if such specified securities are acquired

title: Initial Public Offer by Government Company or Infrastructure Sector SPV
text: to an initial public offer by a government company, statutory authority or corporation or any special purpose vehicle set up by any of them, which is engaged in the infrastructure sector;

source.span_hint: to an initial public offer by a government company

title: Conversion or Exchange of Compulsorily Convertible Securities
text: to equity shares arising from the conversion or exchange of fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities, including depository receipts, that have been held by the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s), as applicable, for a period of at least one year prior to the filing of the draft offer document and such fully paid-up compulsorily convertible securities are converted or exchanged into equity shares prior to the filing of the offer document (i.e., red herring prospectus in case of a book built issue and prospectus in case of a fixed price issue), provided that full disclosures of the terms of conversion or exchange are made in such draft offer document;

source.span_hint: to equity shares arising from the conversion

title: Specified securities ineligible for minimum promoters’ contribution
text: specified securities allotted to the promoters and alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s) during the preceding one year at a price less than the issue price, against funds brought in by them during that period, in case of an issuer formed by conversion of one or more partnership firms or limited liability partnerships, where the partners of the erstwhile partnership firms or limited liability partnerships are the promoters of the issuer and there is no change in the management.
notes: Map to promoter_contribution and entity_types (promoters, AIFs, FVCI, banks, PFIs, insurance companies).

source.span_hint: specified securities allotted to the promoters

title: Eligibility of specified securities for promoters’ contribution under certain schemes
text: Specified securities referred to in clauses (a) and (c) of sub-regulation (1) shall be eligible for the computation of promoters’ contribution if such securities are acquired pursuant to a scheme which has been approved by a High Court or tribunal or the Central Government under sections 230 to 234 of the Companies Act, 2013.

source.span_hint: Specified securities referred to in clauses (a) and (c)

title: Lock-in period for minimum promoters’ contribution
text: minimum promoters’ contribution including contribution made by alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s) referred to in proviso to sub-regulation (1) of regulation 14, shall be locked-in for a period of eighteen months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.
notes: Map to lock_in_period and entity_types (promoters, AIFs, FVCI, banks, PFIs, insurance companies).

source.span_hint: minimum promoters’ contribution including

title: Lock-in period for promoters’ holding in excess of minimum contribution
text: promoters’ holding in excess of minimum promoters’ contribution shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: promoters’ holding in excess of minimum promoters’ contribution

title: Lock-in for SR equity shares
text: The SR equity shares shall be under lock-in until conversion into equity shares having voting rights same as that of ordinary shares or shall be locked-in for a period specified in sub-regulations (1), whichever is later.

source.span_hint: SR equity shares

title: Lock-in for pre-issue capital held by non-promoters
text: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.

source.span_hint: The entire pre-issue capital

title: Exclusion for employee stock options/schemes
text: Nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to equity shares allotted to employees, whether currently an employee or not, under an employee stock option or employee stock purchase scheme or a stock appreciation right scheme of the issuer prior to the initial public offer, if the issuer has made full disclosures with respect to such options or scheme in accordance with Part A of Schedule VI.

source.reg: Lock-in of specified securities held by persons other than the promoters
source.span_hint: equity shares allotted to employees

title: Exclusion for employee stock option trusts
text: Nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to equity shares held by an employee stock option trust or transferred to the employees by an employee stock option trust pursuant to exercise of options by the employees, whether currently employees or not, in accordance with the employee stock option plan or employee stock purchase scheme or a stock appreciation rights scheme.

source.reg: Lock-in of specified securities held by persons other than the promoters
source.span_hint: equity shares held by an employee stock option trust

title: Exclusion for venture capital funds/alternative investment funds
text: Nothing contained in this regulation shall apply to equity shares held by a venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of category I or Category II or a foreign venture capital investor, provided that such equity shares shall be locked-in for a period of at least six months from the date of purchase by the venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of Category I or Category II or foreign venture capital investor.

source.reg: Lock-in of specified securities held by persons other than the promoters
source.span_hint: equity shares held by a venture capital fund

title: Lock-in provisions for stabilising agent under green shoe option
text: The lock-in provisions shall not apply with respect to the specified securities lent to stabilising agent for the purpose of green shoe option, during the period starting from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.

source.reg: Lock-in of specified securities lent to stabilising agent under the green shoe option
source.span_hint: The lock-in provisions shall not apply

title: Lock-in provisions for specified securities lent to stabilizing agent
text: The lock-in provisions shall not apply with respect to the specified securities lent to stabilising agent for the purpose of green shoe option, during the period starting from the date of lending of such specified securities and ending on the date on which they are returned to the lender in terms of sub-regulation (5) or (6) of regulation 57: Provided that the specified securities shall be locked-in for the remaining period from the date on which they are returned to the lender.
notes: Map to lock_in_provisions for green shoe option.

source.span_hint: The lock-in provisions shall not apply

title: Lock-in for partly paid-up specified securities
text: If the specified securities which are subject to lock-in are partly paid-up and the amount called-up on such specified securities is less than the amount called-up on the specified securities issued to the public, the lock-in shall end only on the expiry of three years after such specified securities have become pari passu with the specified securities issued to the public.
notes: Map to lock_in_provisions for partly paid-up securities.

source.span_hint: If the specified securities which are subject

title: Inscription or recording of non-transferability
text: The certificates of specified securities which are subject to lock-in shall contain the inscription “non-transferable” and specify the lock-in period and in case such specified securities are dematerialised, the issuer shall ensure that the lock-in is recorded by the depository.
notes: Map to non_transferability_inscription for locked-in securities.

source.span_hint: The certificates of specified securities which

title: Pledge conditions for lock-in under clause (a) of regulation 16
text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (a) of regulation 16, the loan has been granted to the issuer company or its subsidiary(ies) for the purpose of financing one or more of the objects of the issue and pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan;
notes: Map to loan_purpose and security_pledge_terms fields.

source.span_hint: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms

title: Pledge conditions for lock-in under clause (b) of regulation 16
text: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms of clause (b) of regulation 16 and the pledge of specified securities is one of the terms of sanction of the loan.

source.span_hint: if the specified securities are locked-in in terms

title: Transferability of locked-in specified securities
text: Subject to the provisions of Securities and Exchange Board of India (Substantial Acquisition of shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, the specified securities held by the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 16, may be transferred to another promoter or any person of the promoter group or a new promoter and the specified securities held by persons other than the promoters and locked-in as per regulation 17, may be transferred to any other person holding the specified securities which are locked-in along with the securities proposed to be transferred: Provided that the lock-in on such specified securities shall continue for the remaining period with the transferee and such transferee shall not be eligible to transfer them till the lock-in period stipulated in these regulations has expired.
notes: Map to promoter_transfer and security_lock_in fields.

source.span_hint: Subject to the provisions of Securities

title: Appointment of lead managers
text: The issuer shall appoint one or more merchant bankers, which are registered with the Board, as lead manager(s) to the issue.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall appoint one or more

title: Rights, obligations and responsibilities of lead managers
text: Where the issue is managed by more than one lead manager, the rights, obligations and responsibilities, relating inter alia to disclosures, allotment, refund and underwriting obligations, if any, of each lead manager shall be predetermined and be disclosed in the draft offer document and the offer document as specified in Schedule I.

source.span_hint: Where the issue is managed by more than

title: Non-associate lead manager requirement
text: At least one lead manager to the issue shall not be an associate (as defined under the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Merchant Bankers) Regulations, 1992) of the issuer and if any of the lead manager is an associate of the issuer, it shall disclose itself as an associate of the issuer and its role shall be limited to marketing of the issue.

source.span_hint: At least one lead manager to the issue

title: Appointment of other intermediaries
text: The issuer shall, in consultation with the lead manager(s), appoint other intermediaries which are registered with the Board after the lead manager(s) have independently assessed the capability of other intermediaries to carry out their obligations.

source.span_hint: The issuer shall, in consultation with the

title: Agreements with lead managers and intermediaries
text: The issuer shall enter into an agreement with the lead manager(s) in the format specified in Schedule II and enter into agreements with other intermediaries as required under the respective regulations applicable to the intermediary concerned: Provided that such agreements may include such other clauses as the issuer and the intermediaries may deem fit without diminishing or limiting in any way the liabilities and obligations of the lead
